name: Cherry Springs State Park Rustic Campground
note: Cherry Springs State Park is nearly as remote and wild today as it was two centuries ago. Its dark skies make it a haven for astronomers. Due to its exceptionally dark skies, Cherry Springs State Park is one of the best places on the eastern seaboard for stargazing and the science of astronomy

name: Watchman Campground
note: This high-elevation Zion National Park campground nestled at the base of the iconic Watchman Peak offers dramatic views of red sandstone cliffs. The towering formations create a breathtaking backdrop, especially at sunrise or sunset.

name: North Rim Campground
note: Perched on the quieter, less-visited North Rim at high elevation, this campground provides jaw-dropping vistas of the Grand Canyon's vast expanse. The elevation and remoteness enhance the sweeping views of the canyon's layered cliffs.

name: Silver Bell
note: High-elevation campground located near Aspen, framing the iconic Maroon Bells—twin peaks rising above Maroon Lake. The rugged Elk Mountains and golden aspens in fall make it a postcard-worthy spot.  Not lakefront.

name: Kirk Creek Campground
note: Perched high above the Pacific Ocean along the rugged Big Sur coast, this spot offers RV sites with panoramic views of waves crashing against the cliffs below.  --AWH

name: Many Glacier Campground
note: High-elevation campground with stunning views of Glacier's iconic mountains.  Several campsites are right on the creek.  A lake is nearby.

name: Tuolumne Meadows Campground
note: High in the Sierra Nevada, this Yosemite campground features sweeping views of granite domes, meadows, and waterfalls.  Site A028 is the only RV site that backs up directly to the river.

name: Kalaloch Campground
note: Olympic National Park campground overlooking the Pacific Ocean, this campground provides dramatic coastal views from bluff-top sites.  --AWH

name: Kayenta Campground
note:
High-elevation campground just outside Arches and Canyonlands, overlooking the Colorado River's dramatic 2,000-foot cliffs. The sunrise casts a fiery glow over the desert landscape, rivaling the Grand Canyon in grandeur.
Campground on a mesa top far above the Colorado River; not at river level.

name: Juniper Springs Campground

name: Paynetown Campground

name: Houchin Ferry Campground

name: Savanna Portage State Park Campground
note:
East of the Mississippi in northern Minnesota, this campground overlooks shimmering lakes surrounded by dense woods. The peaceful water views and vibrant autumn colors stand out.

name: Davidson River Campground

note: USACE campground on a peninsula on Carlyle Lake's eastern shore.  Many sites are waterfront with lake views.  --AWH

name: Hickory Point Campground (Sangchris Lake State Park)
note: Illinois DNR campground on Sangchris Lake.  Many RV sites directly on the water with shoreline access.  --AWH

name: Union Bay Campground (Porcupine Mountains Wilderness State Park)
note: State park campground on Lake Superior.  Many sites are right on the lake.  --AWH

name: Port Crescent State Park
note: State park on 3 miles of sandy Saginaw Bay (Lake Huron) shoreline.  Front-row sites are directly on the beach.  --AWH

name: Blackhawk Park
note: USACE campground on Mississippi River Pool 9 back channels near De Soto.  Several sites right on the channel.  --AWH

name: Lake Gogebic State Park
note: State park on Michigan's largest UP lake.  About 20 sites right on the water.  --AWH

name: Burt Lake State Park
note: Popular state park on the southeast shore of Burt Lake.  Many sites are directly on the lakeshore.  --AWH

name: Mitchell State Park
note: Multiple campsites on a canal that connects Lake Cadillac and Lake Mitchell.  Sites are set back behind a towpath and a fence.  --AWH

name: Indian Lake State Park
note: State park on Indian Lake in the central UP.  Sites right on the lake with direct waterfront access.  --AWH

name: Merrick State Park
note: Small state park on Mississippi River backwater channels.  Several sites are right on the water with direct river access.  --AWH

name: Young State Park
note: State park on the north arm of Lake Charlevoix.  Multiple sites are directly on the lakeshore.  --AWH

name: Clear Lake State Park
note: Several campsites are within sight of the lake but view may be partially obstructed.  --AWH

name: Otsego Lake State Park
note: Multiple sites are directly on the lakeshore.  --AWH

name: Wayne Fitzgerrell State Recreation Area
note: Illinois DNR recreation area on Rend Lake (USACE reservoir).  Multiple sites are directly on the lakeshore.  --AWH

name: Patoka Lake
note: Several sites are directly on the lake.  Vegetation may partially obstruct view in summertime.  --AWH

name: Interlochen State Park
note: Several sites on Green Lake are directly on the lakeshore; summer vegetation may block the view.  --AWH

name: South Higgins Lake State Park
note: Most sites are set back from the lake but may still have a partial lake view.  --AWH

name: Lithia Springs Campground (Lake Shelbyville)
note: USACE campground on Lake Shelbyville.  Multiple sites are directly on the lakeshore.  --AWH

name: McLain State Park
note: State park on the Keweenaw Peninsula with campsites directly on the Lake Superior shoreline.  --AWH

name: Aloha State Park
note: Multiple sites are directly on the lakeshore with water views and lake access.  --AWH

name: J.W. Wells State Park
note: On the Green Bay shore of Lake Michigan with several sites directly on the waterfront.  --AWH

name: Clinton Lake State Recreation Area
note: Many sites are on the lakeshore but vegetation may block lake view and access.  --AWH

name: Mississinewa Lake
note: Park is on the lake but campground is not waterfront.  --AWH

name: Salisbury Beach State Reservation
note: In the dunes where the Merimack river meets the Atlantic.  --AWH

name: Horseneck Beach State Reservation
note: In the dunes where Buzzards Bay meets the Atlantic.  --AWH

name: Scusset Beach State Reservation
note: In the dunes where the Cape Cod canal meets Cape Cod Bay.  --AWH

name: Hampton Beach State Park RV Campground
note: In the dunes where the Hampton River meets the Atlantic.  --AWH
